note: Catalog entries are NOT deep-researched records. They exist so every job is findable. Pay/retirement/transition/crosswalk data deliberately absent rather than invented.

specialties.name: Ophthalmologist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role provide a full spectrum of care, including routine eye exams, diagnosis and medical treatment of eye disorders and diseases.

specialties.name: Gynecologic Surgeon and Obstetrician
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role provide prenatal and postnatal care and perform deliveries in maternity cases.

specialties.name: Otolaryngologist - Head and Neck Surgeon
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role examine, diagnose and treat injuries and disorders of the head and neck.

specialties.name: Surgeon
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role treat diseases and injuries by surgical means.

specialties.name: Urologist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role examine, diagnose and treat injuries and disorders of the genitourinary tract.

specialties.name: Nursing Administrator
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role evaluate qualifications and maintain functional control of Air Force Nurse Corps officers, enlisted nursing personnel, civilian nurses and ancillary nursing personnel.

specialties.name: Flight Nurse
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role provide essential care ranging from injury prevention and disease to birth and death.

specialties.name: Emergency/Trauma Nurse
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role provide care that ranges from birth to life- and limb-saving measures.

specialties.name: Neonatal Intensive Care Nurse
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role attend high-risk deliveries.

specialties.name: Obstetrical Nurse
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role may care for high-risk OB patients, level II nursery patients, circulate for cesarean sections or add medical-surgical patients on a single unit inpatient care ward.

specialties.name: Critical Care Nurse
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role operate state-of-the-art equipment on the ground and in the air to assist those who are critically injured.

specialties.name: Mental Health Nurse
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role work with individuals, families, groups and communities to assess mental health needs.

specialties.name: Operating Room Nurse
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role provide professional nursing care to patients undergoing surgery.

specialties.name: Family Nurse Practitioner
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role care for patients of all ages, from infants to adults.

specialties.name: Pediatric Nurse Practitioner
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role provide primary healthcare to pediatric clients.

specialties.name: Women's Healthcare Nurse Practitioner
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role provide primary healthcare to both obstetric and gynecologic patients.

specialties.name: Certified Nurse Midwife
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role provide comfort, support and expertise to patients during pregnancy, labor, delivery and the postpartum period.

specialties.name: Certified Registered Nurse Anesthetist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role administer anesthetics to patients and monitor their care during surgery.

specialties.name: Adult Psychiatric & Mental Health Nurse Practitioner
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role provide comprehensive psychiatric mental healthcare to Airmen, their family and retirees between the ages of 18 and 65.

specialties.name: Orthodontist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role examine, diagnose and treat irregularities of the bite and jaw.

specialties.name: Oral and Maxillofacial Pathologist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role examine and diagnose pathologic conditions of the oral and perioral tissues and structures.

specialties.name: Endodontist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role examine, diagnose and treat diseases and destructive processes, including injuries and abnormalities of dental pulps and periapical tissues of teeth.

specialties.name: Dentist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role maintain the dental health of Airmen and their families.

specialties.name: Periodontist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role examine, diagnose and treat diseases and destructive processes of hard and soft tissues of the teeth.

specialties.name: Pediatric Dentist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role examine, diagnose and treat diseases, abnormalities, injuries and dysfunctional disorders of the oral cavity and its associated structures in children.

specialties.name: Prosthodontist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role restore debilitated dentitions caused by tooth loss or pathologic wear.

specialties.name: Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeon
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role examine, diagnose and treat abnormalities of oral and maxillofacial structures.

specialties.name: Aerospace Medicine Physician Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role conduct medical examinations and provide care for air and missile crews.

specialties.name: Judge Advocate Officer - JAG (Attorney)
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role formulate legal policies.

specialties.name: Chaplain
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role conduct worship services, liturgies and rites.

specialties.name: Behavioral Sciences/Human Factors Scientist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role conduct and manage research on human dynamics of service activities and analyze subsequent results.

specialties.name: Chemist/Nuclear Chemist
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role coordinate research efforts with various agencies.

specialties.name: Physicist/Nuclear Engineer
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role conduct and manage research and experiments.

specialties.name: Developmental Engineer
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role conduct design studies and manage studies contracted to industries.

specialties.name: Acquisition Manager
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role coordinate with commanders and staff to develop proper acquisition plans and schedule.

specialties.name: Contracting Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role formulate contracting policies and procedures.

specialties.name: Financial Management Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role perform financial management functions.

specialties.name: Cost Analysis Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role coordinate cost-analysis programs and activities.

specialties.name: Special Investigations Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Airmen in this role formulate special investigations policies in counterintelligence, cyber threats, counter threat, criminal, fraud and assigned special agents.

sources.title: Air Force Careers - Browse Careers (career finder JSON API, offset/limit paginated)
sources.note: Authoritative first-party airforce.com career-finder API discovered via the data-api-endpoint attribute on the /careers/ page's career-finder component. Returned 234 career entries total (233 after removing one exact duplicate row) covering enlisted AFSCs, officer AFSCs, and officer-corps medical/dental/nursing/chaplain specialty codes, each with AFSC code, track, MAGE composite minimum, career-field tag, and a 'what you'll do' duty-bullet list. Fetched in 3 paginated calls (offset=0/100/200, limit=100, the server's apparent max page size).

sources.title: Air Force Careers landing page (HTML, used to discover the API endpoint)

unverified: Clearance level is reported only for the 7 AFSCs where the airforce.com API's own requirementsTable/Qualifications text explicitly names a clearance (14FX, 1T0X1, 3E8X1, 1N0X1, 1N4X2, 1N4X1, 1N3X1). Many other AFSCs in this catalog (e.g. most of the Intelligence and Cyber Intelligence tag entries) are commonly understood to require Secret or Top Secret/SCI clearance, but the airforce.com career-finder payload does not expose a structured clearance field for those entries, so clearance is left null rather than filled from general knowledge, per the no-fabrication constraint.

The 'what_it_is' one-liner for 4 AFSCs (1Z1X1, 1Z2X1, 1Z3X1, 1Z4X1 -- Pararescue, Combat Control, TACP enlisted, and Special Reconnaissance, respectively) was written from stable, well-established general knowledge rather than the airforce.com API, because those career-finder pages did not return an informationCard 'what you'll do' bullet list in the fetched payload (likely a different page template for Special Warfare pages). Their code, track, tag, and ASVAB/MAGE score are still airforce.com-sourced. Double-check: re-fetch https://www.airforce.com/careers/special-warfare-and-combat-support/special-warfare/pararescue/ (and sibling URLs) directly in a browser to see if the bullet content renders client-side.

For all 'what_it_is' sentences built from airforce.com bullets, the app's cluster-mapping (interest_cluster_ids) is this session's judgment call layered on top of the site's own broader navigational 'tag' grouping (e.g. deciding that an E-composite avionics AFSC belongs in both aviation and technology-cyber, or that Air Force Chaplain/Honor Guard/Religious Affairs -- which have no matching cluster in interest-taxonomy.json's 14 clusters -- map to business-admin as the closest available institutional-support fit). This mapping is a reasoned inference, not a value read from a source, and should be spot-checked against interest-taxonomy.json if cluster assignment precision matters downstream.

asvab values for a handful of officer-track AFSCs (11UX/1U1X1 Remotely Piloted Aircraft Pilot, 2A3X3 Tactical Aircraft Maintenance, 43HX Public Health Officer, 61BX Behavioral Sciences/Human Factors Scientist, 71SX/7S0X1 Special Investigations Officer) are reported as sourced from the site's 'score' field even though ASVAB/MAGE is not typically the gating exam for commissioned officers (AFOQT is) -- these are passed through as-is because they were present in the authoritative payload, but their real-world applicability to officer accession is unconfirmed. One clearly implausible outlier (32EX Civil Engineer's 'G5', far below every other observed composite floor of 21+) was suppressed to null rather than propagated.

233 entries were built from 234 raw API rows after removing one exact duplicate (52RX Chaplain appeared twice). This is very likely not literally every AFSC that has ever existed or every narrow shredout/prefix variant in the full Air Force Enlisted/Officer Classification Directory (AFECD/AFOCD) -- it is every distinct career page airforce.com's own recruiting career-finder currently enumerates, which is the most complete authoritative live source located within this session's tool budget. AFECD/AFOCD were not independently fetched this session (not confirmed reachable within budget); if a primary-source AFECD/AFOCD PDF is later obtained, cross-checking total counts and shredout-level detail against it is the natural next step.
completion_accountability: 233 Air Force specialty entries catalogued (131 enlisted, 102 officer/officer-corps-specialty), covering every career field named in the task instructions: operations/aircrew (pilots, CSOs, RPA pilots/sensor operators, battle management, air traffic control, airfield management), maintenance (aircraft, avionics, munitions, vehicle, facilities/civil engineering), logistics (materiel management, transportation, fuels, contracting), support/admin (personnel, finance, knowledge ops), medical (61 distinct enlisted medical AFSCs plus ~60 officer Medical/Dental/Nurse/Biomedical Sciences/Medical Service Corps specialty codes), legal (paralegal, JAG), chaplain, intelligence (all-source, signals, geospatial, cryptologic, targeting), cyber (cyber warfare ops, cyber systems ops, cyber transport, cyberspace effects/ops officer), space (space systems ops, space operations officer, missile/space maintenance), special warfare (Pararescue, Combat Control, Special Reconnaissance, TACP enlisted and officer, Combat Rescue Officer, Special Tactics Officer, SERE, EOD), security forces (enlisted and officer), public affairs, and band (enlisted band, Premier Band, Band Officer). Source: a single authoritative first-party API (airforce.com's own career-finder JSON endpoint), fully paginated (3 calls) rather than sampled, so this reflects airforce.com's complete current career list rather than a partial scrape. Data NOT independently cross-checked against the primary AFECD/AFOCD directory (that fetch was not attempted this session; see unverified). 0 career fields named in the task instructions were skipped. Clearance is populated only where explicitly stated in the source text (7 of 233 entries); left null elsewhere rather than filled from general knowledge. Tool budget used: roughly 15 fetch/curl calls against airforce.com (2 HTML page fetches to locate the API endpoint, several limit/offset probes to find the server's page-size cap, then 3 full-data paginated calls), well under the ~25-call budget.
